title: Start Planned Project Task
content:
Feature: Start Planned Project Task in Salesforce PSA

Page URL: https://ukgsf--stest.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/page/home

Login Flow

* Navigate to https://ukgsf--stest.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/page/home

* Click SSO Login if required

* Authenticate using the organization's SSO provider

* User is redirected to the Salesforce PSA Home page

Acceptance Criteria

AC1. After successful SSO login, the Salesforce PSA Home page is displayed.

AC2. The global search is available on the Home page.

* User can search using the project name.

* Search is case-insensitive.

AC3. Searching for the project name:

* "360 HEALTH AND COMMU - Q-383470 - Ready Fixed Fee Implementation"
returns the matching project in the search results.

AC4. Clicking the project from the search results opens the Project Detail page.

AC5. The Project Detail page displays the Project Tasks link.

AC6. Clicking Project Tasks opens the Project Tasks page showing all tasks associated with the selected project.

AC7. The Project Tasks page displays tasks with their current status.

* If one or more tasks have status Planned, they are available for selection.

* If no Planned task exists, an appropriate message is displayed or the test is marked as not applicable.

AC8. Clicking a Project Task with status Planned opens the Project Task Detail page.

AC9. The Project Task Detail page displays a Start button when the task status is Planned.

AC10. Clicking the Start button changes the Project Task status from:

* Planned → Started

AC11. The updated status is displayed immediately on the Project Task Detail page after the action is completed.

User Flows

Flow 1 — Login using SSO

1. User navigates to the Salesforce PSA URL.

2. User clicks the SSO Login button.

3. User successfully authenticates.

4. Salesforce PSA Home page is displayed.

Flow 2 — Search Project

1. User enters the project name:
"360 HEALTH AND COMMU - Q-383470 - Ready Fixed Fee Implementation"

2. Search results are displayed.

3. User selects the matching project.

Flow 3 — Open Project Tasks

1. Project Detail page opens.

2. User clicks the Project Tasks related link.

3. Project Tasks page is displayed.

Flow 4 — Open Planned Project Task

1. User reviews the Project Task list.

2. User selects a task whose status is Planned.

3. Project Task Detail page opens.

Flow 5 — Start Project Task

1. User clicks the Start button.

2. System updates the task status.

3. Status changes from Planned to Started.

4. Updated status is displayed on the Project Task Detail page.

Edge Cases

* Invalid project name returns No matching projects found.

* Project exists but contains no Project Tasks.

* Project Tasks exist but none have status Planned.

* Multiple Planned tasks exist; user can start the selected task independently.
